check.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$apps=@('excel.exe','groove.exe','lync.exe','msaccess.exe','mspub.exe','onenote.exe','outlook.exe','powerpnt.exe','visio.exe','winproj.exe','winword.exe'); $roots=@($env:ProgramFiles,${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}) | Where-Object { $_ }; $installed=$apps | Where-Object { $app=$_; $roots | Where-Object { Test-Path (Join-Path $_ ('Microsoft Office\root\Office16\' + $app)) -or Test-Path (Join-Path $_ ('Microsoft Office\Office16\' + $app)) } }; $key='HKLM:\SOFTWARE\MICROSOFT\INTERNET EXPLORER\MAIN\FEATURECONTROL\FEATURE_HTTP_USERNAME_PASSWORD_DISABLE'; $bad=$installed | Where-Object { $props=Get-ItemProperty -Path $key -Name $_ -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $props.PSObject.Properties[$_].Value -ne 1 }; if (($installed | Measure-Object).Count -gt 0 -and -not $bad) { 'Compliant' }"

candidate_check.description: User name and password must be disabled in all Office programs.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Disable user name and password" is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_HTTP_USERNAME_PASSWORD_DISABLE

If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
fix_text_excerpt: Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Disable user name and password" to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for  all installed Office programs.
title: User name and password must be disabled in all Office programs.
